title: Filling a provider's models from the free-stack sources: never fabricate a missing fact, ground copy in facts, and know that this skill only writes the editorial half
summary: The DF.1 pilot filled the 5 genuine OpenAI models (gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4-turbo, o1, o1-mini) end-to-end and surfaced the judgment calls a catalog author (or a future curator agent) hits. (1) SCOPE: author-catalog writes COPY only — description/tags/badges/links. The factual fields (context_window, max_output, pricing, capabilities, gate_catalog_benchmarks, gate_catalog_provider_perf) are filled by the ENRICHMENT path (OpenRouter/LLM-Stats/Wikidata → the discovery sync), not by this skill. A description on a stub row (context 0, no benchmarks) is not a filled model; author copy AFTER the facts land. (2) LICENSING: descriptions are composed from structured facts, NEVER copied from vendor prose / model cards / Wikipedia (copyright + CC-BY-SA share-alike) — the same class of defect as an unattributed link. (3) MISSING DATA is not fabricated: a delisted model (o1-mini, dropped from OpenRouter) has no primary spec source, so its context/max_output stay a visible GAP and pricing is backfilled per-field from a secondary sourced value (LLM-Stats provider row), never guessed. A source that returns nothing (Wikidata resolved no QID for all 5 because it was searched with the ugly display_name = spidergate_id 'gpt-4o-openai' instead of a canonical name) leaves that field empty, flagged, not filled. (4) SELF-REPORTED benchmarks (vendor's own numbers, self_reported=TRUE on the row) are honest to store but must be labeled as vendor-reported, not independent. (5) The provider bucket can be polluted: provider='openai' had 14 rows that were actually GLM/Qwen/Kimi/MiniMax/Doubao mislabeled — enumerate the GENUINE set before filling. Take-away for the future curator agent: fetch + provenance is automatable; descriptions/badges need human/LLM authoring; every write keeps facts + our words + provenance, and a gap is left visible, never invented.
references: GateBoard DF.1 (task b8f426ad, PR #2373) — the transparent OpenAI catalog-fill pilot; docs/external/catalog-fill-pilot-openai-2026-07-10.md is the full per-field source table + judgment calls (§3) + the DF.2 source→field recipe (§4)

app/services/gate/catalog_enrichment/{service.py,normalize.py,sources/{openrouter,llm_stats,wikidata}.py} — the free-stack connectors + the compose_description/normalize_score helpers the pilot reused (the FACTS half, separate from this skill's COPY half)

SpiderGate LEARNINGS 2026-07-10 — 'Catalog enrichment matches + composes off display_name, which for configured rows IS the spidergate_id' (Wikidata resolved nothing) + 'the enrichment agent does NOT write the flat spec columns, and provider=openai is polluted with 14 mislabeled rows'

provider_metadata PATCH /admin/gate/providers/{name}/metadata (app/api/v1/admin_gate/providers.py) — the provider-editorial endpoint the pilot filled (description + free_tier_description), documented in references/author-provider.md
